Media Object
Media Object base API
Media C API Documentation

Introduction

The Media C API defines the base C interface for creating and controlling media processing elements. It is a pure-C, header-only library designed to be embedded into concrete implementations that provide the actual media processing logic through virtual function pointers.

The API is consumed directly by C code or serves as the foundation for higher-level wrappers such as the Transform C API.

Architecture Overview

A media_t element is a generic media component that exposes one or more streams. Each element has at least one stream (sink or src). The Media C API is intended to build media components. Depending on its stream configuration, an element can be:

  • A source – only src (output) streams: produces data and abstracts the underlying data source (capture device, file reader, network input, etc.).
  • A sink – only sink (input) streams: consumes data (e.g. display, encoder).
  • A transform – both sink and src streams: receives, processes, and outputs data.

Each stream is a stream_t with a direction_t and a set of capabilities_t describing the supported formats.

Below are the three possible topologies:

Source element (src streams only):

Transform element (sink + src streams):

Sink element (sink streams only):

Lifecycle State Machine

Every media_t instance follows this state machine:

Public API Reference

Lifecycle

FunctionDescription
media_initialize()Transition from Null to Initialized.
media_prepare()Transition from Initialized to Prepared. Resources are allocated.
media_finalize()Transition from Prepared to Initialized. Resources are freed.
media_release()Transition from Initialized to Null. Final cleanup.
media_get_state()Query the current lifecycle state.

Information & Inspection

FunctionDescription
media_about()Retrieve element metadata (name, description, version, etc.).
media_get_version()Get the implemented media API version.
media_inspect()Print a human-readable dump of the element.

Streams & Capabilities

FunctionDescription
media_get_streams()List all input/output streams.
media_get_stream_capabilities()Get current capabilities of a stream.
media_set_stream_capabilities()Set desired capabilities on a stream.
media_query_stream_capabilities()Query compatible capabilities in the current config context.
media_query_memory_allocation()Query memory constraints for a stream's input buffers.

Controls

FunctionDescription
media_get_controls()List all available controls.
media_get_control()Read the current value of a control.
media_set_control()Write a new value to a control.

Implementing a Media Element

To create a concrete media element, populate every function pointer in the media_t structure. These callbacks form the virtual interface that the public media_* wrapper functions call internally.

Lifecycle Hooks

Function pointer Purpose
do_initialize Acquire resources needed to operate (hardware handles, internal structures).
do_prepare Allocate processing resources and validate the configuration.
do_finalize Free resources allocated during prepare.
do_release Release all resources and return to the Null state.

Information

Function pointer Purpose
get_version Return the implemented API version.
about Return element metadata (name, description, …).
get_last_error Return diagnostic details about the last error.

Streams & Capabilities

Function pointer Purpose
get_streams Return the list of exposed streams.
do_get_stream_capabilities Return the capabilities of a given stream.
do_set_stream_capabilities Accept and apply the negotiated capabilities on a stream.
query_stream_capabilities Answer dynamic capability queries based on current config.
do_query_memory_allocation Provide buffer memory constraints for a stream.

Controls

Function pointer Purpose
get_controls Return the list of available controls.
do_get_control Read the current value of a named control.
do_set_control Apply a new value to a named control.

Depending on the stream configuration the element can behave as:

  • A source — only output streams: the implementation produces data (e.g. camera capture, file reader).
  • A sink — only input streams: the implementation consumes data (e.g. display, file writer).
  • A transform — both input and output streams: extend with the Transform C API and implement do_process_stream as well.

Core Data Types

TypeHeaderDescription
media_tmedia.hMain handle embedding the virtual interface.
stream_tstream.hDescribes an I/O stream with direction and capabilities.
streams_tstreams.hDynamic list of stream_t.
capabilities_tcapabilities.hDynamic list of properties_t* (format sets).
control_tcontrol.hDescribes a single configurable parameter.
controls_tcontrols.hDynamic list of control_t.
property_tproperty.hNamed key-value pair.
properties_tproperties.hDynamic list of property_t.
value_tvalue.hTagged variant holding a typed value.
buffer_tbuffer.hMemory blocks + timestamp + metadata.
memory_tmemory.hRaw contiguous memory block descriptor.
memories_tmemories.hDynamic list of memory_t.
stream_buffer_tstream_buffer.hAssociates a stream name with a buffer.
stream_buffers_tstream_buffers.hDynamic list of stream_buffer_t.
fraction_tfraction.hRational number (numerator / denominator).
list_tlist.hGeneric dynamic array used by all typed lists.
media_state_tstate.hLifecycle state enumeration.
Media C error codeserror.hError code values.

Error Handling

All functions return int (one of media_error_codes). Zero (MEDIA_ERROR_NONE) indicates success; negative values indicate errors. See error.h for the full list.

Generated by  1.12.0
